title: agrostis boormanii
intro: Agrostis boormanii, also known as Boorman's bent or bent grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Agrostis boormanii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antiviral and antifungal properties. Agrostis boormanii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional Maori medicine to treat a range of ailments.
